title: NOC 80020 – Managers in agriculture | GTR Immigration
description: Managers in agriculture under NOC 80020 are the visionary leaders of Canada’s multi-billion dollar agri-food industry. As a TEER 0 (Management) occupation, these professionals are responsible for the strategic planning, production, and marketing functions of farms, ranches, and orchards. In 2026, as the sector pivots toward "Smart Farming" and enhanced environmental sustainability, these managers are critical in integrating digital technology with traditional cultivation and breeding. For immigration, this role is a top-tier candidate for Express Entry and specialized provincial pathways like the Saskatchewan Agriculture Talent Pathway.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category8 – Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupationsTEER Level0 (Management)Major Group80 – Management occupations in natural resources, agriculture and related productionSub-Major Group800 – Management occupations in natural resources, agriculture and related productionMinor Group8002 – Managers in agriculture, horticulture and aquacultureNOC Code80020

duties: Plan, organize, and direct the overall operations of a farm, ranch, or orchard.
Determine the types and quantities of crops to be grown and livestock to be raised.
Coordinate planting, cultivating, and harvesting activities as well as livestock breeding cycles.
Hire, train, and manage farm personnel and agricultural laborers.
Develop marketing programs and establish supply chain contracts for farm products.
Maintain comprehensive financial and production records for business sustainability.
Oversee the purchase and maintenance of high-tech farm machinery and equipment.
employment_requirements: Extensive Farming Experience: Usually obtained as a farm supervisor or specialized worker, this is the primary requirement.
Academic Credentials: A university degree or college diploma in agricultural management, crop production, or animal science is often required for large-scale corporate farms.
Technical Proficiency: Knowledge of modern digital technology and sustainable farming equipment is increasingly essential.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. While the number of individual farms is consolidating, the size and complexity of remaining operations are increasing. According to recent Job Bank data, the need for professional managers to oversee large-scale "Agri-Corporations" will remain high through 2027, with retirements being the primary driver of new job openings.
labour_market_demand: The demand for agricultural managers is Good, driven by the consolidation of smaller farms into large-scale corporate operations and the retirement of aging farm owners. In 2026, Ontario (especially the Annapolis Valley and Niagara regions) and Saskatchewan are showing the highest demand. With over 32,000 managers currently active in Ontario alone, the sector remains a cornerstone of the Canadian economy.
Global Agri-Food Hub: Canada is a world leader in pulse, grain, and livestock production, offering managers exposure to international trade.
Technological Advancement: Lead the implementation of drone-based crop monitoring, automated harvesting, and AI-driven livestock health tracking.
Sustainability Focus: Play a key role in Canada’s 2026 climate initiatives by managing sustainable soil and water usage.
Entrepreneurial Opportunities: Nearly 79% of managers in this NOC are self-employed or own their establishments.
pathways: Saskatchewan Agriculture Talent Pathway: A specialized stream for agricultural workers with a job offer in the province.
Express Entry (Federal Skilled Worker): High CRS points for TEER 0 management experience.
Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP): Targeted draws for those with experience in the agri-food sector.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: Priority for managers supporting regional food security and processing.
